title: Brainstorm / Design
goal: Use Superpowers brainstorming to clarify the real goal, constraints, alternatives, risks, and success criteria before coding agents change the system.
artifactEvidence: Success criteria ready for a spec-driven handoff
failurePrevented: Coding agents start from a vague prompt and build the wrong thing.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: Ask what data arrives, who consumes it, what freshness matters, and what failure behavior is acceptable before proposing implementation.

antipatterns: Coding before clarifying the outcome
Treating the first implementation idea as the design
doneCriteria: The user has approved the design direction, scope, and success criteria.

title: Specification
goal: Convert the approved Superpowers design into a spec-driven contract with observable behavior, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and risks.
failurePrevented: Coding agents implement from memory and miss requirements that were only implied.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: Specify source formats, schema drift behavior, retry policy, data quality checks, and the evidence required before the pipeline can be called complete.

antipatterns: Writing tasks before behavior is specified
Leaving edge cases for coding agents to invent
doneCriteria: Every requirement has observable behavior or verification evidence.

title: Workspace Isolation
goal: Use Superpowers workspace isolation to create a spec-driven safe branch or worktree, inspect repository state, and prevent coding agents from trampling unrelated edits.
failurePrevented: Coding agents mix another person's edits into the task or use destructive git cleanup.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: Create an isolated workspace for the pipeline task, record existing repository changes, and keep unrelated dashboard or artifact edits out of the diff.

antipatterns: Working from a dirty shared branch without checking status
Resetting files that belong to someone else
doneCriteria: Coding agents can name the exact workspace and unrelated changes that must remain untouched.

title: Implementation Planning
goal: Break the spec-driven contract into ordered, testable implementation steps with files, verification commands, and review checkpoints.
failurePrevented: Coding agents jump into a broad feature and lose track of dependencies or acceptance criteria.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: Plan separate tasks for schema parsing, validation, storage writes, error handling, and end-to-end verification instead of one giant pipeline change.

antipatterns: Planning one oversized build-it-all task
Skipping file-level scope and test commands
doneCriteria: Each task is independently understandable, testable, and tied back to the specification.

title: Execution
goal: Use Superpowers execution discipline so coding agents follow the spec-driven plan, update progress, handle blockers explicitly, and avoid unauthorized scope expansion.
failurePrevented: Coding agents implement later redesign work or unrelated rendering changes while completing a data-only task.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: Execute the planned parser, validation, and storage tasks in order, stopping for clarification if an acceptance criterion is missing.

antipatterns: Changing simulation or visual design while executing a data task
Treating blockers as permission to guess
doneCriteria: The completed diff maps directly to the assigned implementation-plan task and nothing more.

title: Test-Driven Development
goal: Apply Superpowers spec-driven TDD by writing a focused failing test before production changes, confirming the RED failure, then implementing the smallest change that makes it GREEN.
artifactEvidence: Regression test committed with the behavior
failurePrevented: Coding agents write code first and later add tests that only mirror the implementation.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: First write a failing test for rejected malformed records or schema drift, verify the failure, then implement the minimal pipeline behavior.

antipatterns: Accepting syntax errors as RED
Adding broad abstractions before a test requires them
doneCriteria: The test failed for the expected reason before implementation and passes after the minimal change.

title: Systematic Debugging
goal: Use Superpowers spec-driven systematic debugging to reproduce failures, gather evidence, isolate root cause, and verify the fix against the original symptom.
failurePrevented: Coding agents guess at changes and accidentally mask the real issue.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: When duplicate records appear, reproduce the case, inspect idempotency keys and retry logs, then add a targeted test before changing pipeline logic.

antipatterns: Changing multiple variables at once
Calling a rerun success a fix for a flaky failure
doneCriteria: The root cause is explained with evidence and the original failure path is verified fixed.

title: Code Review
goal: Use Superpowers spec-driven review workflows to request and receive rigorous feedback focused on correctness, regression risk, spec alignment, and missing tests.
artifactEvidence: Review request with scope and evidence
failurePrevented: Coding agents treat review as style feedback and miss behavioral or verification gaps.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: Ask reviewers to check schema handling, retry semantics, observability, and test coverage against the ingestion-pipeline specification.

outputs: Accepted or rejected feedback with rationale
Updated tests or code where needed
antipatterns: Blindly applying unclear feedback
Reviewing formatting while ignoring missing failure cases
doneCriteria: High-severity findings are fixed or explicitly deferred with technical rationale.

title: Verification
goal: Use Superpowers spec-driven verification to run fresh commands, read outputs, check requirements directly, and only then report what is passing or incomplete.
failurePrevented: Coding agents say the work should pass without current evidence.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: Run unit, integration, and representative end-to-end checks for the pipeline, then report exact pass/fail counts and unresolved risks.

antipatterns: Relying on earlier test output
Ignoring warnings or partial failures
doneCriteria: Every completion claim is backed by fresh command output or explicitly marked unverified.

title: Branch Completion
goal: Use Superpowers spec-driven branch completion to commit only scoped changes, report exact verification evidence, and prepare the branch for PR, merge, or follow-on tasks.
failurePrevented: Coding agents commit unrelated files or claim completion without a traceable diff and test record.

example.task: Need to build an ingestion pipeline
example.superpowersMove: After verification, commit only the pipeline files, include the requested message, and report the hash plus exact tests run.

antipatterns: Committing local artifacts
Mixing the next task into the completion commit
doneCriteria: The branch contains a scoped commit with exact changed files, test evidence, and no unrelated edits.
